article_title: First Scientifically Accurate Skeleton of Deinosuchus schwimmeri Goes on Display in Georgia After 40-Year Hunt

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: First Scientifically Accurate Skeleton of Deinosuchus schwimmeri Goes on Display in Georgia After 40-Year Hunt
content_preview.summary: The 31-foot Late Cretaceous dinosaur-eating crocodilian has been reconstructed in full for the first time using 3D scans of fossils collected over four decades, with the only mounted cast unveiled at the Tellus Science Museum.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A 31-foot crocodilian that hunted dinosaurs across the Late Cretaceous coastline of what is now the southeastern United States has been reconstructed as a full mounted skeleton for the first time. The cast of *Deinosuchus schwimmeri*, unveiled at the Tellus Science Museum in Cartersville, Georgia, is the only scientifically accurate, life-size replica of the species in the world, [according to ScienceDaily](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/04/260415043623.htm). The reconst...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong Science & Research News piece (~626 words, in News range). Clean structure (Overview / What We Know / Why a Full Mount Matters / What We Don't Know). Tone neutral and scholarly.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 3 local snapshots. Every claim verifies: 31-foot D. schwimmeri / Tellus Cartersville / Schwimmer's 40-year fieldwork / 2020 species naming / 83-76 Mya range / 9.45m / Tellus only display (ScienceDaily); two-year Triebold collaboration / Alabama+Georgia+Texas sites / Schwimmer 'blueprint' quote / 3D-scan dermal-armor methodology (SciTechDaily); Smithsonian+AMNH+Tellus repositories / 40-mile Columbus radius / Wagar+Melsheimer+Roney installers / 2002 King of the Crocodylians / 2010 bite mark + coprolite studies / 2020 JVP paper (phys.org).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verify cleanly. Minor note: phys.org's installation photo caption calls Melsheimer 'Cultural Coordinator' while body text uses 'curatorial coordinator' — the article uses the body-text version (correct). Hannah Eisla as director of education is verified in phys.org body text.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — no prior MH coverage of Deinosuchus or this exhibit.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean APPROVE. Excellent paleontology piece, every claim sourced verbatim. Ready to merge.
